authority_contract.rule: LLM repair loops are allowed when a general criterion has objective machine evidence: PASS/FAIL/DIFF, coverage gap, lint diagnostic, assertion failure, stale evidence, traceability gap, or measured performance gap. Human gates own intent, semantic rule changes, waivers, interface changes, performance tradeoffs, and final signoff.

general_evaluation_criteria.criterion: Every generated artifact traces to SSOT requirement, function_model, cycle_model, interface, coverage, or quality_gates refs.
RTL observable behavior matches FunctionalModel results for every required transaction/scenario.
Each behavior-owning RTL module has module-boundary evidence before top-level signoff.
SSOT coverage goals are hit by passing RTL-observed scoreboard evidence.
Ports, handshakes, ordering, reset, and error protocol obey SSOT io_list/cycle_model/interface contract.
DUT-only RTL compile/lint has zero unwaived errors, warnings, and style diagnostics.
Simulation results are fresh, bounded, non-stale, and tied to structured scoreboard goals.
Cycle/performance targets from cycle_model/timing are measured or explicitly escalated.
Waveforms and debug probes are sufficient to inspect reset, interface activity, state, outputs, and failures.
Generated RTL/TB is structured by SSOT-owned modules, avoids fixed IP templates, and remains reviewable.
Intent, semantic rule changes, waivers, interface changes, performance tradeoffs, and final signoff are human-owned.

locked_artifacts.name: requirement
locked_artifacts.change_rule: Human owns intent, scope, priority, tradeoffs, and final acceptance.

locked_artifacts.name: ssot_spec
locked_artifacts.change_rule: Behavior, interface, coverage goal, and waiver changes require human gate.

locked_artifacts.name: functional_model
locked_artifacts.change_rule: FunctionalModel is the golden oracle; sim-debug cannot change it to match RTL.

locked_artifacts.name: coverage_plan
locked_artifacts.change_rule: LLM may add tests to hit bins, but cannot weaken or delete bins without approval.

locked_artifacts.name: interface_contract
locked_artifacts.change_rule: Protocol and port contract changes require human approval.

locked_artifacts.name: performance_target
locked_artifacts.change_rule: LLM may sweep candidates, but target/tradeoff changes require human approval.

loopable_evidence_points.criterion: SSOT refs vs generated artifact refs
loopable_evidence_points.llm_action: Add missing refs/artifact ownership or open a human gate if ownership is undefined.

loopable_evidence_points.criterion: FunctionalModel.apply expected values vs RTL observed values
scope.level=module equivalence goal against RTL module boundary observations
loopable_evidence_points.llm_action: Patch the owning RTL module only.

loopable_evidence_points.criterion: coverage goals vs RTL-observed scoreboard coverage rows
loopable_evidence_points.llm_action: Add stimulus/tests/vectors without changing coverage goals.
Patch RTL syntax, widths, drivers, and coding style.
Patch RTL or TB driver/monitor depending on classified owner.
Run parameter/architecture sweeps and report tradeoff candidates.

loopable_evidence_points.criterion: minimal vector still reproduces the same failure
scoreboard diff, logs, coverage miss, and waveform evidence
loopable_evidence_points.llm_action: Write root-cause report and repair prompt.

loopable_oracles.criterion: SSOT refs vs generated artifact refs
loopable_oracles.llm_action: Add missing refs/artifact ownership or open a human gate if ownership is undefined.

loopable_oracles.criterion: FunctionalModel.apply expected values vs RTL observed values
scope.level=module equivalence goal against RTL module boundary observations
loopable_oracles.llm_action: Patch the owning RTL module only.

loopable_oracles.criterion: coverage goals vs RTL-observed scoreboard coverage rows
loopable_oracles.llm_action: Add stimulus/tests/vectors without changing coverage goals.
Patch RTL syntax, widths, drivers, and coding style.
Patch RTL or TB driver/monitor depending on classified owner.
Run parameter/architecture sweeps and report tradeoff candidates.

loopable_oracles.criterion: minimal vector still reproduces the same failure
scoreboard diff, logs, coverage miss, and waveform evidence
loopable_oracles.llm_action: Write root-cause report and repair prompt.

non_loopable_decisions: change performance target or architecture tradeoff

goals.title: Transaction reset_architecture matches FunctionalModel

observables: All externally visible output valids and interrupt are deasserted during reset.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/ordering must hold when applicable
state_updates: All architectural state returns to declared reset values.

pass_criteria: Scoreboard calls FunctionalModel.apply with the same transaction intent
RTL observed outputs equal FL expected outputs
RTL side effects match FL model state updates
Linked functional coverage bins are hit when stimulus executes

goals.title: Transaction apb_register_write matches FunctionalModel

constraints: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and pready == 1

observables: pready acknowledges the access; pslverr reports illegal address/access/strobe.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/ordering must hold when applicable
state_updates: Writable register fields update only through declared write_effect rules; reserved fields ignore writes.
expected_contract.error_policy: {'id': 'ERR_APB_ILLEGAL', 'condition': 'illegal_apb_access == 1', 'result': 'pslverr asserted for the completing access; architectural DMA transfer state is not modified.'}

pass_criteria: Scoreboard calls FunctionalModel.apply with the same transaction intent
RTL observed outputs equal FL expected outputs
RTL side effects match FL model state updates
Linked functional coverage bins are hit when stimulus executes

goals.title: Transaction apb_register_read matches FunctionalModel

constraints: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 0 and pready == 1

observables: prdata returns the decoded register value with reserved bits forced to zero.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/ordering must hold when applicable
state_updates: APB reads do not alter architectural state.

pass_criteria: Scoreboard calls FunctionalModel.apply with the same transaction intent
RTL observed outputs equal FL expected outputs
RTL side effects match FL model state updates
Linked functional coverage bins are hit when stimulus executes

goals.title: Transaction single_or_multi_beat_memory_copy matches FunctionalModel

observables: Each successful beat writes the captured read data to the destination address.
Final successful beat sets status COMPLETED and raises the channel-complete pending bit.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/ordering must hold when applicable
state_updates: SAR and DAR increment by DATA_WIDTH/8 bytes after each successful write response.
loop_remaining decrements after each successful write response.
expected_contract.error_policy: {'id': 'ERR_AXI_RD', 'condition': 'rvalid == 1 and rready == 1 and rresp != 0', 'result': 'status=FAULTED, error_code=ERR_AXI_RD, CH_FAULT pending set, no write for the failed beat.'}; {'id': 'ERR_AXI_WR', 'condition': 'bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp != 0', 'result': 'status=FAULTED, error_code=ERR_AXI_WR, CH_FAULT pending set.'}

pass_criteria: Scoreboard calls FunctionalModel.apply with the same transaction intent
RTL observed outputs equal FL expected outputs
RTL side effects match FL model state updates
Linked functional coverage bins are hit when stimulus executes

goals.title: Transaction wait_for_peripheral_event matches FunctionalModel

observables: Channel remains WAITING_FOR_PERIPHERAL until the selected event bit is sampled high.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/ordering must hold when applicable
state_updates: No AXI transaction is issued while selected_event is zero.

pass_criteria: Scoreboard calls FunctionalModel.apply with the same transaction intent
RTL observed outputs equal FL expected outputs
RTL side effects match FL model state updates
Linked functional coverage bins are hit when stimulus executes

goals.title: Transaction fault_completion matches FunctionalModel

observables: Fault status is latched and a channel-fault pending interrupt is set.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/ordering must hold when applicable
state_updates: First fault wins until software clears INTSTATUS and restarts the channel.
expected_contract.error_policy: {'id': 'ERR_UNALIGNED', 'condition': 'addresses_aligned == 0', 'result': 'status=FAULTED and error_code=ERR_UNALIGNED.'}; {'id': 'ERR_DEBUG_REJECT', 'condition': 'debug_execute == 1 and manager_busy == 1', 'result': 'debug command rejected without transfer side effects.'}

pass_criteria: Scoreboard calls FunctionalModel.apply with the same transaction intent
RTL observed outputs equal FL expected outputs
RTL side effects match FL model state updates
Linked functional coverage bins are hit when stimulus executes

goals.title: Transaction interrupt_write_one_to_clear matches FunctionalModel

constraints: psel == 1 and penable == 1 and pwrite == 1 and pready == 1

observables: dmac_irq reflects the enabled pending status after the W1C clear.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/ordering must hold when applicable
state_updates: Only bits written as one are cleared; bits written as zero retain their prior value.

goals.title: Scenario SC_RESET_APB: Reset and APB discovery

constraints: Assert/deassert dmacresetn, read DBGSTATUS/CSR/INTSTATUS, and attempt reserved address accesses.

observables: Reset values match registers/function_model; reserved fields read zero; illegal APB access asserts pslverr only for the access.
All externally visible output valids and interrupt are deasserted during reset.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/handshake constraints apply to the scenario
state_updates: All architectural state returns to declared reset values.

pass_criteria: Generated sequence executes the SSOT stimulus
Scoreboard expected result comes from FunctionalModel.apply and SSOT scenario expected field
RTL observed result matches expected result
Scenario coverage bin is hit

goals.title: Scenario SC_SINGLE_BEAT_COPY: Single-beat DMA memory copy

constraints: Program SAR/DAR/LOOP_CFG with loop_count=0, enable completion interrupt, start channel, and return OKAY read/write responses without backpressure.

observables: One read beat is written unchanged to DAR, SAR/DAR increment by 8, status reaches COMPLETED, CH_COMPLETE pending is set, and dmac_irq asserts if enabled.
Each successful beat writes the captured read data to the destination address.
Final successful beat sets status COMPLETED and raises the channel-complete pending bit.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/handshake constraints apply to the scenario
state_updates: SAR and DAR increment by DATA_WIDTH/8 bytes after each successful write response.
loop_remaining decrements after each successful write response.
expected_contract.error_policy: {'id': 'ERR_AXI_RD', 'condition': 'rvalid == 1 and rready == 1 and rresp != 0', 'result': 'status=FAULTED, error_code=ERR_AXI_RD, CH_FAULT pending set, no write for the failed beat.'}; {'id': 'ERR_AXI_WR', 'condition': 'bvalid == 1 and bready == 1 and bresp != 0', 'result': 'status=FAULTED, error_code=ERR_AXI_WR, CH_FAULT pending set.'}

pass_criteria: Generated sequence executes the SSOT stimulus

Each successful beat writes the captured read data to the destination address.
Final successful beat sets status COMPLETED and raises the channel-complete pending bit.

observables: Payload signals remain stable while valid waits for ready; architectural state updates only on completed handshakes.

goals.title: Scenario SC_WFP_EVENT: Wait-for-peripheral event gating

constraints: Set CONTROL.wfp_enable and wfp_event, start a channel while selected event is low, then assert the selected event.

observables: Channel remains WAITING_FOR_PERIPHERAL with no AXI issue until event is sampled, then resumes transfer.
Channel remains WAITING_FOR_PERIPHERAL until the selected event bit is sampled high.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/handshake constraints apply to the scenario
state_updates: No AXI transaction is issued while selected_event is zero.

constraints: Start a transfer and return non-OKAY rresp on the read data channel.

observables: Channel transitions to FAULTED, error_code=ERR_AXI_RD, CH_FAULT pending set, no destination write for the failed beat.
Fault status is latched and a channel-fault pending interrupt is set.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/handshake constraints apply to the scenario
state_updates: First fault wins until software clears INTSTATUS and restarts the channel.

goals.title: Ordering rule ordering_0

constraints: A write data beat must not be issued before the corresponding read data beat has been accepted into rd_buf.

expected_contract.latency: {'register_read': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 1, 'description': 'pready/prdata/pslverr complete in APB access phase.'}, 'register_write': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 1, 'description': 'Writable side effects occur on the completing APB access cycle.'}, 'event_release': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 2, 'description': 'Selected peripheral event sampled then WFP state can advance.'}, 'single_beat_transfer': {'min_cycles': 5, 'max_cycles': None, 'description': 'Command accept, AR, R, AW/W, and B stages; max is unbounded under AXI backpressure.'}, 'interrupt_assertion': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 2, 'description': 'Pending enabled interrupt reflects terminal status or W1C clear within two cycles.'}}

goals.title: Ordering rule ordering_1

constraints: A channel-complete interrupt pending bit is set only after the final successful B response.

goals.title: Ordering rule ordering_2

constraints: A channel-fault interrupt pending bit is set on the first detected fault before any later completion status.

expected_contract.latency: {'register_read': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 1, 'description': 'pready/prdata/pslverr complete in APB access phase.'}, 'register_write': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 1, 'description': 'Writable side effects occur on the completing APB access cycle.'}, 'event_release': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 2, 'description': 'Selected peripheral event sampled then WFP state can advance.'}, 'single_beat_transfer': {'min_cycles': 5, 'max_cycles': None, 'description': 'Command accept, AR, R, AW/W, and B stages; max is unbounded under AXI backpressure.'}, 'interrupt_assertion': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 2, 'description': 'Pending enabled interrupt reflects terminal status or W1C clear within two cycles.'}}

pass_criteria: RTL response order matches cycle_model ordering
FL trace and RTL monitor trace preserve the same transaction order

goals.title: Ordering rule ordering_3

constraints: APB W1C clear does not clear configuration registers or address counters.

expected_contract.latency: {'register_read': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 1, 'description': 'pready/prdata/pslverr complete in APB access phase.'}, 'register_write': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 1, 'description': 'Writable side effects occur on the completing APB access cycle.'}, 'event_release': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 2, 'description': 'Selected peripheral event sampled then WFP state can advance.'}, 'single_beat_transfer': {'min_cycles': 5, 'max_cycles': None, 'description': 'Command accept, AR, R, AW/W, and B stages; max is unbounded under AXI backpressure.'}, 'interrupt_assertion': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 2, 'description': 'Pending enabled interrupt reflects terminal status or W1C clear within two cycles.'}}

pass_criteria: RTL response order matches cycle_model ordering
FL trace and RTL monitor trace preserve the same transaction order

goals.title: Ordering rule ordering_4

constraints: For each channel, at most one read burst and one write burst are outstanding in this engineering subset.

expected_contract.latency: {'register_read': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 1, 'description': 'pready/prdata/pslverr complete in APB access phase.'}, 'register_write': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 1, 'description': 'Writable side effects occur on the completing APB access cycle.'}, 'event_release': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 2, 'description': 'Selected peripheral event sampled then WFP state can advance.'}, 'single_beat_transfer': {'min_cycles': 5, 'max_cycles': None, 'description': 'Command accept, AR, R, AW/W, and B stages; max is unbounded under AXI backpressure.'}, 'interrupt_assertion': {'min_cycles': 1, 'max_cycles': 2, 'description': 'Pending enabled interrupt reflects terminal status or W1C clear within two cycles.'}}

goals.title: Error policy None

goals.title: Module pl330realverify_regs functionality equals FunctionalModel

scope.boundary: module RTL observable outputs must be compared against FunctionalModel.apply

constraints: Use the module boundary stimulus/monitor contract from SSOT decomposition; do not compare against RTL-internal self-derived expected values.

observables: All externally visible output valids and interrupt are deasserted during reset.
expected_contract.latency: cycle_model latency/ordering must hold at the module boundary
state_updates: All architectural state returns to declared reset values.

pass_criteria: Drive the RTL module boundary with stimulus derived from the same SSOT transaction intent used for FunctionalModel.apply
Observe only RTL module boundary outputs/state exposed by SSOT or rtl_contract
Compare RTL observed values against FunctionalModel.apply output; do not copy FL expected into rtl_observed
